text_blocks.text: Commander in Chief of the Army and
Navy of the United States. He has the
authority to command them to take
appropriate military action in the event of a
sudden crisis. However, only the Congress is
explicitly granted the power to declare war
per se, as well as to raise, fund and
maintain the armed forces. Congress also has
the duty and authority to prescribe the
laws and regulations under which the armed
forces operate, such as the Uniform Code
of Military Justice, and requires that all
Generals and Admirals appointed by the
president be confirmed by a majority vote
of the Senate before they can assume their
